_comment: FTSE 250 index level at most recent London close. The FTSE 250 is a cleaner read on the domestic UK economy than the FTSE 100 (which is dominated by global commodity and pharma names). LSEG does not expose a free index-level JSON feed; the number is transcribed from the published closing price each week. The adapter's freshness guard rejects fixtures older than 14 days so a missed refresh surfaces in the ingest audit. 2026-07-13 refresh: last completed session close is Friday 10 July 2026 = 23371.4 (+0.56% on the day), taken from the Yahoo Finance ^FTMC daily series -- the same free proxy the ftse-250-history back-series uses (Yahoo prints can drift ~1% from the LSEG closing-auction print). Live prod path uses EODHD FTMC.INDX; NOTE: EODHD began returning HTTP 402 Payment Required for the FTMC.LSE symbol around 2026-07-02 (plan/coverage change) -- if the .INDX symbol is also plan-gated the fixture remains the operative path until the EODHD subscription question is settled. TODO(source): an authorised LSEG index-licence vendor would let us drop this fixture and fetch the close directly.
release_schedule: Every London trading day at ~16:30; editorial fixture refreshed weekly.
